stemHtml: What is meant by the OCA of a precision approach?

options.a: The lowest altitude, below which an aircraft must initiate a missed approach if visual references cannot be established.
options.b: The lowest altitude or the elevation of the relevant runway threshold, if the threshold elevation is more than 2 m (7 ft) below the aerodrome elevation, below which an aircraft cannot descend without infringing the appropriate obstacle clearance criteria.
options.c: The lowest altitude above the aerodrome elevation below which an aircraft cannot descend without infringing the appropriate obstacle clearance criteria.
options.d: The lowest altitude at which a missed approach must be initiated to ensure compliance with the appropriate obstacle clearance criteria.
